feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Is it possible to query dimension and measure metadata in Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hello, Everyone 

Is there a way to query metadata on measures and dimensions in SAP datasphere? Specifically the business/technical names of the measures and dimensions, and what view they belong to. 

Right now i'm extracting this data into snowflake through the OData relational API, just to reimport it back into datasphere. It seems stupid to extract data from datasphere just to import back into the same system. 
 

link to the API i referred to:
SAP Datasphere: Analytical and Relational OData AP... - SAP Community

entry.title: RealTime replication IFICOMPANYCODE
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm looking for a way to do a RealTime replication of IFICOMPANYCODE (based on a remote table).

But, as stated in the documentation, this is not supported on ABAP tables. Not being an expert in SAP tables I'm running out of ideas.

How can I find a CDS view that has CDC capabilities based on this ABAP definition, because the IFICOMPANYCODE is already a join of multiple other tables.

 

KR, Harry

entry.title: In Datasphere Analytic Model, can I have two structures -- one in rows and one in columns?
entry.content:
In Datasphere, I want to build an Analytic Model with two structures -- one in rows and one in columns.   This can be done in SAP BW, but is it possible in Datasphere.

In rows, I will have a restricted key figures based on a key figure (measure) and specific non-date related dimension values and calculated key figures that have formulas based on the restricted key figures given on the rows.

In columns,  I will have a restricted key figures based on specific date related dimension values (current fiscal year, previous fiscal years, etc.) and calculated key figures that have formulas based on the restricted key figures given on the columns.

Is this possible?

Thanks.

Regards,

Mel Calucin

entry.title: Delta Load from One Datasphere Space to Another (Data Lake Type) – Data Not Overwriting Properly
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue with delta loads across SAP Datasphere spaces and would appreciate your guidance.

Scenario Overview

Step 1: BW Bridge Space → Space A

  • We are performing delta data loads from a view in BW Bridge space to a local table in Datasphere Space A using a Transformation Flow.

  • The BW Bridge view is shared with Space A.

  • The local table in Space A is delta-enabled.

  • Load type used: Initial and Delta.

✅ Result:

  • Delta loads are running successfully.

  • Data in Space A matches the data in BW Bridge space

 

Step 2: Space A → Space B (Data Lake Type)

  • The delta-enabled local table in Space A is shared with Space B.

  • Space B is of type Data Lake.

  • A Transformation Flow is created in Space B to load delta data from the shared table (Space A) into a delta-enabled local table in Space B.

  • Load type used: Initial and Delta.

⚠️ Issue:

  • Delta loads are running without errors.

  • The number of delta records loaded from:

    • BW Bridge → Space A

    • Space A → Space B
      are the same.

  • However, data in Space B is not matching Space A.

  • Records are not overwriting properly in Space B.

  • Data is not in sync.

 

Troubleshooting Already Attempted

  • Executed Merge after data load in Space B.

  • Performed table optimization steps in Space B.

  • Issue still persists.

 

Question

Am I doing anything wrong while implementing delta loads from one Datasphere space to another, especially when the target space is of type Data Lake?

Is there any specific consideration for:

  • Delta handling behavior in Data Lake spaces?

  • Primary key / semantic key handling?

  • Overwrite vs append behavior?

  • Merge requirements in Data Lake tables?

  • Delta propagation limitations across spaces?

Any guidance or best practices for this scenario would be greatly appreciated

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - While Selective Deletion in table and Restricted Variable in Analytics Model
entry.content:
Hi guys,

While working with Analytic Model I faced this issue, I've one Manual Input Restricted Variable with filter type: - Interval used in one of Restricted Measure, and I've one column "Product_ID"  it consist P1 to P21 product.

when I preview my data and set my value to P14 to P20, then in output I'm having P2 also which shouldn't be in this range, initially I thought it is a bug or glitch.

Input: - 

Output: -

But when i tried selective deletion in table ( Delete Filtered Records ). then I got somewhat similar output, I've 2 column ID and Name and ID when I try to delete record from 1-5 then filtered record should be ( 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ) but instead I'm getting ( 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 11 )

Data in table: - 

Input for data filter: -

output:- 

acording to this filter the data is not fetched properly so im sure this is bug/glitch 

is anyone else have faced this issue.???

Let me know if there is smtg else or this bug arrives in this quarterly update.

entry.title: Data Replication from GTS to SAP Business data cloud
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have an existing SAP Global Trade Services system and are planning to integrate it with SAP Business Data Cloud.

Our objective is to bring GTS data into SAP Datasphere, combine it with other enterprise and external datasets (such as weather and cost indices), and leverage Databricks for predictive analytics and forecasting.

Could you please clarify:

  • What is the recommended data movement architecture from GTS to Datasphere and further into Databricks for predictive use cases? We came across Hana data lake files which can be embedded into Datasphere and we can move data in parquet or csv format which can act as source to Databricks.
  • Whether data needs to be physically replicated into Databricks or can be accessed virtually?
  • How data storage is handled across Datasphere (BDC) and Databricks, and whether this results in data duplication?
  • What are the key cost components involved, specifically for:
    • BDC/Datasphere storage and processing (CU-based)
    • Databricks storage and compute (DBU-based)
    • Data movement or integration costs
    • What are the best practices to optimize storage and compute costs for such predictive scenarios?

      Any help would be appreciated

entry.title: SAP DATASPHERE
entry.content:
How to export plan data from datasphere to s/4 hana ?

SAP Datasphere SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Delta enablement in bw tables to expose it in sap datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Guys,

Now to migrate our bw system to sap datasphere we have 2 ways to transfer the tables direct connection / BW bridge, and bridge is used to convert non-hana optimise obj to hana optimise, or to preserve the existing logic of BW.

but my real question is like how the BW tables are exposed for delta usage in dataphere, i mean how the CDC work for tables if we need that table as delta enabled, we know that in CDS, CDC is available but for tables I actually dk in BW we dont don't have any spefic mechanism beside SLT.
and only standard DSO/Standard ADSO have change log what about master info object?

entry.title: Datasphere hierarchy -- parent node having its own value and not the sum of child nodes and itself.
entry.content:
I'm migrating a BW query to Datasphere.   The BW query has a structure hierarchy.  Unfortunately, Datasphere Analytic Model doesn't support structure hierarchy.  This is per note 3695233 - Structure hierarchy like in BW query cannot be used in Datasphere Analytic Model - SAP for Me.

I'm trying to see if a Datasphere dimension hierarchy can solve this problem.

Below is the dimension Profit Analysis in a flat presentation:

Below is the Profit Analysis dimension with the hierarchy selected.   

Note that Node "Label 4" in the hierarchy mode has the value of $5246 which is the sum of Label 4 through Label 13 -- I don't want this.  I want Label 4 value to be what it is in the flat presentation which is $305.    Is there a way to do this?

Thanks.

Regards,

Mel Calucin

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

More on this:

So I went to the Fact Table that is being used by the Profit Analysis Analytic Model and changed the Amount measure to have an aggregation of NONE instead of SUM.

This resulted in the parent nodes on the Analytic Model to now have * instead of a value:

I'm going to propose that instead of showing a '*' when the measure's aggregation setting is set to NONE that it should have the ACTUAL value of dimension hierarchy parent node.

Created Customer Influence Improvement Request -- dimension parent child hierarchies with directories -- change measure determination of parent node

Please vote on above enhancement request.

Thanks.

Regards,

Mel Calucin

entry.title: How to pass longtext via extraction enable CDS to Datasphere
entry.content:
I need to pass longtext in datasphere using extraction enabled CDS. However, neither longtext nor table functions can be used for it.
Could you please to identify the best way to do it.

Regards,
Malika Bathla.

SAP Datasphere SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: How can we write back data from SAP Datasphere to S4HANA On-premise?
entry.content:
Hi,

Would like to check what are the options to write back data from SAP Datasphere to S4HANA On-premise?

Use Case: to write back 3rd party data such as exchange rate data to S4HANA On-premise

Thank you.

XQ

entry.title: How does delta works for Datasphere Transformation Flow?
entry.content:
Hello,

At the moment, I do not have sufficient time to conduct a proof of concept (POC) in our DSP system, as I am currently engaged with other priorities. However, in preparation for future DSP-related tasks, I would like to understand how delta handling works in a Transformation Flow.

Scenario:
We have three Local Tables populated via Replication Flows:

  • LT1 – EKKO
  • LT2 – EKPO
  • LT3 – EKBE

These Local Tables are joined in a Graphical View based on defined business logic. The resulting View is then persisted (staged) into a new Local Table.

Question:
How does the delta mechanism behave for a Transformation Flow that extracts data from a View and loads it into a Local Table?

Note: I have not yet conducted a POC or performed detailed research on this topic.

entry.title: SFTP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I understand in SAP Datasphere (SAP BDC) Trail version we can not create SFTP connection. How ever I tried to prepare steps in sequence as prerequisites to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere . Hope this will be helpful for understanding purpose.

I request community members, to let me know if I have any mistakes in below steps and if there is any way to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere in trail version.

From your Laptop Start Menu à Operational features

 

 

Check OpenSSH Client might already available

 

To install SSH Server click on Add a features

In search bar type Open --> OpenSSH Server --> Install

 

 

Goto Control Panel --> Click on Windows Defender Firewall --> Advanced Settings --> Inbound rules --> New Role

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

From search bar search for Services

Try to find OpenSSH Server --> Right Click Properties

General Tab --> Startup Type = Automatic and click on Start        

Apply , OK and close window

 

 

 

Install WinSCP

From Google download WinSCP for windows --> go for official website

Run the .exe file for all users

Go with Default installation (Typical installation, Comander , install)

Select only check box Launch WinSCP --> Finish

As it will launch WinSCP

File Protocol = SFTP

 

SAP Cloud Connector

Log in SAP Cloud Connector: https://localhost:8443/

Add Subaccount

Goto Cloud and you can leave Host, Port, User and Password as blank

Click on Next

 

Configure Manually

 

 

 

 

In trail Version you can select Region as US East (VA) – AWS only. Other wise you can select any region. I copied Region and Subaccount from SAP BTP Cockpit as shown below screen shot. Normally in paid version you can select as per license

Login E-Mail/ID – This is of SAP BTP and Password also BTP.

 

SAP BTP Cockpit

 

 

Log on to SAP Cloud Connector à Select Subaccount crated in SAP Cloud Connector

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Cloud Connector à SFTP

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

How to get Host Key

Before Going to SAP Datasphere,

Goto Windows Power Shell and type below syntax

ssh-keyscan -p 22 sftp.XXX.XXX.X.X. hostkey.txt (in place of XXX.XXX.X.X – give your IP address)

Then it will open a notepad with Key.

But you need to take the last line in genearted Host Key from the note pad as like below

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

SAP Datasphere à Connections

 

 

 

 

 

Enter Host Key and give WinSCP Host details (SFTP)

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

 

 

We get below error in Free Trail Version

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Missing Steps

In SAP Datasphere (BDC) Trail version we don’t have access to System à Administration

 

 

 

If it gets success

Datasphere → BTP → Cloud Connector → SFTP is working.

entry.title: SFTP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I understand in SAP Datasphere (SAP BDC) Trail version we can not create SFTP connection. How ever I tried to prepare steps in sequence as prerequisites to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere . Hope this will be helpful for understanding purpose.

I request community members, to let me know if I have any mistakes in below steps and if there is any way to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere in trail version.

From your Laptop Start Menu à Operational features

 

 

Check OpenSSH Client might already available

 

To install SSH Server click on Add a features

In search bar type Open --> OpenSSH Server --> Install

 

 

Goto Control Panel --> Click on Windows Defender Firewall --> Advanced Settings --> Inbound rules --> New Role

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

From search bar search for Services

Try to find OpenSSH Server --> Right Click Properties

General Tab --> Startup Type = Automatic and click on Start        

Apply , OK and close window

 

 

 

Install WinSCP

From Google download WinSCP for windows --> go for official website

Run the .exe file for all users

Go with Default installation (Typical installation, Comander , install)

Select only check box Launch WinSCP --> Finish

As it will launch WinSCP

File Protocol = SFTP

 

SAP Cloud Connector

Log in SAP Cloud Connector: https://localhost:8443/

Add Subaccount

Goto Cloud and you can leave Host, Port, User and Password as blank

Click on Next

 

Configure Manually

 

 

 

 

In trail Version you can select Region as US East (VA) – AWS only. Other wise you can select any region. I copied Region and Subaccount from SAP BTP Cockpit as shown below screen shot. Normally in paid version you can select as per license

Login E-Mail/ID – This is of SAP BTP and Password also BTP.

 

SAP BTP Cockpit

 

 

Log on to SAP Cloud Connector à Select Subaccount crated in SAP Cloud Connector

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Cloud Connector à SFTP

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

How to get Host Key

Before Going to SAP Datasphere,

Goto Windows Power Shell and type below syntax

ssh-keyscan -p 22 sftp.XXX.XXX.X.X. hostkey.txt (in place of XXX.XXX.X.X – give your IP address)

Then it will open a notepad with Key.

But you need to take the last line in genearted Host Key from the note pad as like below

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

SAP Datasphere à Connections

 

 

 

 

 

Enter Host Key and give WinSCP Host details (SFTP)

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

 

 

We get below error in Free Trail Version

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Missing Steps

In SAP Datasphere (BDC) Trail version we don’t have access to System à Administration

 

 

 

If it gets success

Datasphere → BTP → Cloud Connector → SFTP is working.

entry.title: No possibility to register FileAdapter in DPAgent 2.8.0 with Datasphere
entry.content:
I have installed and connected DPAgent 2.8.0 with Datasphere. The status is on green in Datasphere and I can register the available Adapters (for instance, ABAP). As it is indicated in note 3487646, it is not indicating the connection to HANA in the Agent with versions over 2.7.4. 

In the list of available Adapters in Datasphere, it is not shown FileAdapter as an option. Then, I try to register it with CLI configuration tool agentcli.bat --configAgent, but the next message appears:

Can anyone help me registering FileAdapter, please?

entry.title: Datasphere AWS S3 Connection to a DTP Object Store Service not possible
entry.content:
I have an AWS S3 Object Store created on our BTP Account. A Service Key has been created. When I try to validate the connection in Datasphere the next message appears:

Cause: Possible causes of error: Detailed error message (user: arn:aws:iam::'user'  is not authorized to perform: s3:listallmybuckets because no identity\-based policy allows the s3:listallmybuckets action (service: amazon s3; status code: 403; error code: accessdenied; request id: 8bg3wzykpdqw7p1m; s3 extended request id: 'id'.

Seeing this message, I want to try to afford the corresponding policies to the user of the Object Store (as indicated in SAP Datasphere - How to configure the AWS Assume Role option for an AWS S3 connection).

Anyone knows how to do it for a BTP Object Store or is there a limitation?

entry.title: Create space button is not available in free trail of SAP Dataspere.
entry.content:
Hi,
I have created a SAP Datasphere trail version and I tried to create Sapce in Datasphere, Create button is not available. I think the trail version i am using might be 30 days one or it would not be advaced trail version. If any other issue please advice me to fix it or if the issue is with trail version please share 90 days trail link or advice me how to fix it.

entry.title: Minimum Roles and Authorization for Technical user for Datasphere Prod Connection
entry.content: We are configuring technical user for Datasphere Prod connection with S4H on premise system and looking for minimum Roles and Authorization for this Technical user relevant to our SAP systems. We cannot provide SAP_ALL access in production. Please suggest SAP Note or KBA.

entry.title: SAP datasphere task chain: sql procedure not visible
entry.content:
I've created an SQL procedure in my Open SQL Schema of SPACE  "SAP_ADMIN.

I'd like to run this procedure in a Task Chain in SAP DSP, in the SPACE_ID = SAP_ADMIN. I'm following this: Run Open SQL Procedures in a Task Chain | SAP Help Portal 

However, even after assigning the privileges to the space as described here: Allow the Space to Access the Open SQL Schema | SAP Help Portal , I'm not able to see the SQL procedure in during Task Chain modelling.

The error showed is: No procedures have been created or the EXECUTE privilege has not been granted to the Open SQL schema. 

Could you please investigate?

entry.title: How to create a report of manage sales prices in SAC using Datasphere model ?
entry.content:
Hi, Actually there is a requirement where I need to create a report in SAC using Datasphere model which shows the details of 'manage sales prices' fiori app of S4HANA Public cloud Edition. Report should show all the Standard prices of Products and also if there is some changes in the prices it show all the change logs along with the user details who did the changes and with the current condition amount. I created a custom CDS in FIORI by utilizing a standard CDS I_SLSPRCGCONDITIONRECORD but this is not holding Product details.

SAP Datasphere  SAP Analytics Cloud  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales
